title: Pinsker/KL identification in secondres (sub-point of Girsanov gap)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where:** proof of `secondres` in `L:27`.

The chain $d_{TV}\le\sqrt{\tfrac12\mathrm{KL}}$ with $\mathrm{KL}\le\tfrac12\int_0^\infty\!\int_D\lambda_t^2\mathbb E|u_t-\tilde v_t|^2$ converts the integrability bound $\le C\|u_0-v_0\|_\infty^{2/9}$ into $d_{TV}\le C\|u_0-v_0\|_\infty^{1/9}$. The exponent bookkeeping ($2/9\to1/9$ via the square root) is internally consistent.

**Gap.** The identification of the Kullback–Leibler divergence of the Girsanov change of measure with $\tfrac12\mathbb E\int\|g_t\|^2$ (relative-entropy = expected quadratic Cameron–Martin energy) is asserted, not derived, and depends on the well-posedness/equivalence-of-laws gap. Provide the explicit relative-entropy computation for this feedback drift.
